title: Phase 3: Arrow-Based Pivot
description:
## Goal
Implement ~100 LOC of Arrow-based pivot algorithm to convert n-dimensional array data to tabular format.

## Tasks
1. Core array-to-table transform algorithm using Arrow
2. Use Arrow buffers directly for efficiency
3. Dictionary encoding for coordinate columns (~75% memory savings)
4. Strided memory access to convert chunk data to row-oriented format

## The Pivot Algorithm
The pivot algorithm converts n-dimensional array data into a tabular format for SQL queries using strided memory access:

For each cell in the output table:
1. Calculate the n-dimensional index from the row position
2. Determine which chunk contains that index
3. Calculate the offset within the chunk
4. Apply strided access to read the value

This is strided memory access - the same technique used by NumPy arrays.

## Key Optimization: Dictionary Encoding
When flattening n-dimensional data, coordinate values repeat many times. Using dictionary encoding instead of plain arrays significantly reduces memory usage:

| Approach | Memory for 10M rows |
|----------|-------------------|
| Plain arrays | ~300 MB |
| DictionaryArray | ~75 MB |

## Dependencies
- Arrow C++: Already a DuckDB dependency

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Can pivot 1D coordinate arrays to table columns
- [ ] Can pivot 2D+ data arrays to table columns
- [ ] Dictionary encoding works for coordinate columns
- [ ] Memory usage is reasonable (<2x raw data)
- [ ] Build passes on CI
